title: Mild Indian Dhal Dal
description: A floral, chewy, and comforting Maharashtrian dish perfect for hangover relief. Serve with steamed basmati rice or roti.

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: use 2 cups for toor dal and 2 cups for moong dal

instructions: Heat ghee in a large pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Let them sizzle for 1 minute.
Add sliced onion and cook until translucent.
Add ginger, garlic, and methi leaves. Cook for 1 minute.
Add toor dal and moong dal. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add chana dal, sugar, and salt. Mix well.
Add 2 cups of water for toor dal and 2 cups of water for moong dal. Bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the dals are cooked and the liquid has reduced.
Stir occasionally to prevent the dal from sticking to the pan.
Once the dal is cooked, mash some of the dal against the side of the pan to create a thick, creamy consistency.
Serve hot with steamed basmati rice or roti.
